note_data.title: Reading — Advanced Comprehension
keyPoints: Comprehension is the ability to understand and grasp the meaning of written text
Critical reading involves analyzing, evaluating and interpreting the author's ideas and arguments
Context clues help determine the meaning of unfamiliar words from surrounding sentences
Main idea is the central point or message that the author wants to convey in a passage
Supporting details are facts, examples or explanations that prove or explain the main idea
Inference means drawing conclusions based on information that is not directly stated
Tone refers to the author's attitude toward the subject matter being discussed
Summary writing requires identifying key points and expressing them in your own words concisely

definitions.definition: The ability to understand and grasp the meaning of written or spoken language
Hints or suggestions found within a sentence, paragraph or passage that help readers understand the meaning of new or unfamiliar words
The most important point or central message that an author is trying to communicate in a paragraph or passage
A logical conclusion drawn from evidence and reasoning rather than from explicit statements in the text
The attitude or feeling that an author expresses toward a subject through choice of words and writing style

importantQuestions.question: What is the difference between main idea and supporting details?
importantQuestions.answer: Main idea is the central message of a passage while supporting details are facts, examples or explanations that prove or explain the main idea.

importantQuestions.question: Explain the importance of context clues in reading comprehension with examples.
importantQuestions.answer: Context clues are essential for understanding unfamiliar words without using a dictionary. They help readers: 1) Determine word meanings from surrounding text 2) Improve reading fluency 3) Enhance vocabulary naturally 4) Better understand author's intended meaning. Examples include definition clues (author defines the word), synonym clues (similar words nearby), antonym clues (opposite words), and example clues (illustrations provided).

mcqs.question: The central message of a passage is called:
Drawing conclusions not directly stated in text is called:
Hints that help understand unfamiliar words are:

commonMistakes: Students confuse main idea with supporting details - always identify the central message first
Not reading the entire passage before answering questions leads to incorrect responses
Ignoring context clues and immediately assuming they don't know word meanings
Writing summaries that are too long - summaries should be 1/3 the length of original text
Mixing personal opinions with author's stated ideas when making inferences
examTips: This chapter appears in every BSEK paper with 2-3 MCQs and 1 long comprehension passage
Always read questions first, then read the passage to save time
Underline key words in questions and find them in the passage
Practice identifying main idea in first and last sentences of paragraphs
note_data.keyDiagram: Reading Process Flowchart: Preview → Read → Question → Summarize → Review - Students must memorize this 5-step reading process
